content:
# John Chapter 10 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/JHN_010_page_001.jpg, JHN_010_page_002.jpg

1. "Amen, amen, I say to you, the [one] not entering through the door into the fold of the sheep, but climbing up from another place, that [one] is a thief and a robber.

2. But the [one] entering through the door is [the] shepherd of the sheep.

3. To this [one] the doorkeeper opens, and the sheep hear the voice of him, and the own sheep he calls by name and leads them out.

4. When he has put out all the own, he goes before them, and the sheep follow him, because they know the voice of him.

5. But a stranger they will by no means follow, but will flee from him, because they do not know the voice of the strangers."

6. This figure of speech the Yeshua spoke to them, but those [ones] did not know what the things were that he was speaking to them.

7. Then the Yeshua said again, "Amen, amen, I say to you, I am the door of the sheep.

8. All who came before me are thieves and robbers, but the sheep did not hear them.

9. I am the door; through me if anyone enters, he will be saved, and will go in and go out and will find pasture.

10. The thief does not come except to steal and kill and destroy; I came that they may have life and have [it] abundantly.

11. I am the good shepherd. The good shepherd lays down the life of him for the sheep.

12. The hired [one], and not being [a] shepherd, whose own the sheep are not, sees the wolf coming and leaves the sheep and flees — and the wolf snatches them and scatters —

13. because he is a hired [one] and it does not matter to him concerning the sheep.

14. I am the good shepherd, and I know the [ones] mine, and the [ones] mine know me,

15. just as the Father knows me and I know the Father; and the life of me I lay down for the sheep.

16. And other sheep I have that are not of this fold; those also I must lead, and they will hear the voice of me, and there will be one flock, one shepherd.

17. Because of this the Father loves me, because I lay down the life of me, that I may take it again.

18. No one takes it from me, but I lay it down from myself. I have authority to lay it down, and I have authority to take it again. This commandment I received from the Father of me."

19. A division again happened among the Ioudaioi because of these words.

20. And many of them said, "He has a demon and is mad. Why do you listen to him?"

21. Others said, "These words are not [those] of a [one] having a demon. Is a demon able to open [the] eyes of [the] blind?"

22. Then the Dedication happened in Hierosolyma. It was winter.

23. And the Yeshua was walking in the temple in the portico of Solomon.

24. Then the Ioudaioi surrounded him and said to him, "Until when do you hold the soul of us [in suspense]? If you are the Christos, tell us plainly."

25. The Yeshua answered them, "I told you, and you do not believe. The works that I do in the name of the Father of me, these testify concerning me.

26. But you do not believe, because you are not of the sheep of me.

27. The sheep of me hear the voice of me, and I know them, and they follow me.

28. And I give to them eternal life, and they will never perish to the age, and no one will snatch them from the hand of me.

29. The Father of me, who has given [them] to me, is greater than all, and no one is able to snatch from the hand of the Father.

30. I and the Father are one."

31. The Ioudaioi again picked up stones that they might stone him.

32. The Yeshua answered them, "Many good works I showed you from the Father; because of which work of them do you stone me?"

33. The Ioudaioi answered him, "Concerning a good work we do not stone you, but concerning blasphemy, and because you, being a man, make yourself God."

34. The Yeshua answered them, "Is it not written in the law of you, 'I said, you are gods'?

35. If he called them gods to whom the word of the God came — and the scripture is not able to be broken —

36. [do you say] of him whom the Father sanctified and sent into the world, 'You blaspheme,' because I said, 'I am [the] Son of the God'?

37. If I do not do the works of the Father of me, do not believe me.

38. But if I do, even if you do not believe me, believe the works, that you may know and understand that the Father [is] in me, and I [am] in the Father."

39. Then they sought again to seize him, and he went out of the hand of them.

40. And he went away again beyond the Iordan to the place where Ioannes was first baptizing, and he remained there.

41. And many came to him and said, "Ioannes indeed did no sign, but all things that Ioannes said concerning this [one] were true."

42. And many believed in him there.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.7 — "I am the door":** The third and fourth "I am" statements with predicates (the door, vv.7,9; the good shepherd, vv.11,14). The image shifts from door to shepherd — Jesus is both the access point and the guardian.

2. **v.8 — "All who came before me":** A difficult saying. "Before me" (πρὸ ἐμοῦ) may mean "in my place" rather than "before my time" — referring to false messiahs and corrupt leaders, not to Moses or the prophets.

3. **v.16 — "Other sheep not of this fold":** The "other sheep" are Gentiles who will believe. "One flock, one shepherd" — not one fold (αὐλή) but one flock (ποίμνη). The unity is in the shepherd, not in a single institutional structure.

4. **v.22 — "The Dedication" (τὰ ἐγκαίνια):** Hanukkah — the feast commemorating the rededication of the temple by Judas Maccabaeus in 164 BCE after its desecration by Antiochus Epiphanes. Jesus is in the temple during a feast celebrating the temple's sanctification — and claims that he himself is the one "the Father sanctified and sent into the world" (v.36).

5. **v.29 — Textual variant:** Sinaiticus appears to read "The Father of me, who has given [them] to me, is greater than all" — ὃ δέδωκέν μοι πάντων μεῖζόν ἐστιν. The meaning: what the Father has given (the sheep) is the greatest gift, and no one can snatch it away.

6. **v.30 — "I and the Father are one" (ἕν):** ἕν is neuter ("one thing"), not masculine ("one person"). Jesus claims unity of purpose and nature with the Father, not that he and the Father are the same person. The Jews nonetheless hear a claim to deity (v.33).

— EMET, AI Translator
